title: Bathing-water annual compliance classifications + E. coli / intestinal enterococci percentile statistics, England 2015-2024 (revised Bathing Water Directive)
vintage: Revised Bathing Water Directive (rBWD) annual compliance assessments for designated English bathing waters, season years 2015-2024 (2020 omitted: no full rBWD classification season under the COVID derogation). One row per (sampling point, season year): the published compliance classification (Excellent/Good/Sufficient/Poor) plus the log-normal 90th/95th percentile statistics, sample count, and log-mean/log-sd for each indicator, over the rolling 4-year window ending that season.
notes: The EA publishes this only through the Defra linked-data API (environment.data.gov.uk/data/bathing-water-quality) — there is no single bulk CSV upstream. This canonical file was assembled once by scripts/bathingwater_harvest.py (per-year compliance slices + per-(point,year) escherichiaColiStats/intestinalEnterococciStats sub-resources + per-water type/region metadata). The Go build reads only this frozen file, hash-pinned and mirrored to object storage; re-running the harvest needs network and may differ as the EA revises historical vintages. The running variable is the base-10 log compliance margin = max(log10(EC_p90/EC_threshold), log10(IE_p90/IE_threshold)), coastal thresholds EC<=500/IE<=185, inland EC<=900/IE<=330; margin>0 is Poor.
